title: 10 best herbal creams for amenorrhea

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai creams helps with amenorrhea because they stimulate the uterus to produce hormones that regulate menstruation. The cream's active ingredients, such as angelica sinensis, work to balance the body's estrogen and progesterone levels, which can become disrupted in cases of amenorrhea. By promoting hormonal balance, dong quai creams can help restore regular menstrual cycles, alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalances, and support overall reproductive health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dong Quai's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances and amenorrhea by promoting a healthy environment for hormone production.

Vasodilatory properties: Dong Quai's ability to dilate blood vessels helps improve circulation, which can increase blood flow to the uterus and ovaries, supporting hormonal balance and regular menstrual cycles.

Estrogen-regulating properties: Dong Quai contains isoflavones that can help regulate estrogen levels in the body, which can be beneficial for women with amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or deficiencies.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots help to balance hormones and regulate menstrual cycles, making them a key component in treating amenorrhea.
Rhyzomes: With their unique chemical composition, rhyzomes have been shown to stimulate blood flow and circulation, which can help alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Prepare angelica sinensis dried root powder by grinding 20g of dried roots into a fine powder.
Mix the powdered angelica sinensis with 100ml of sweet almond oil and 50ml of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted.
Add 10ml of jojoba oil and 5 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and continue heating for an additional 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before transferring it into a sterilized cream jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain the herb Vitex agnus-castus, which has been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles. The cream works by stimulating the pituitary gland to produce more luteinizing hormone (LH), which in turn helps to stimulate ovulation and regulate hormonal imbalances that may be causing amenorrhea. By promoting a healthy balance of hormones, chaste tree creams can help to stimulate regular menstrual bleeding and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Vasoprotective effects: Chaste tree cream's vasoprotective effects help to strengthen and stabilize blood vessels, which can improve uterine blood flow and hormonal regulation, thereby addressing underlying causes of amenorrhea.

Hormone-balancing properties: The cream contains compounds that have a regulating effect on the endocrine system, particularly on estrogen and progesterone levels, helping to restore normal menstrual cycles in cases of amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Chaste tree cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the uterus and ovaries, which may contribute to amenorrhea caused by conditions such as endometriosis or polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in iridoid glycosides, particularly ajugoside and aucubin, which have been shown to stimulate the uterus and regulate menstrual cycles.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds that help to balance female hormones and alleviate symptoms of Amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Grind 50g of dried vitex agnus castus fruit into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered vitex with 200ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler heated to 60°c for 30 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let it steep for 4 hours allowing the mixture to infuse and combine ingredients.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to stimulate the pituitary gland and hypothalamus, regulating hormonal imbalances that can cause amenorrhea. The licorice root also possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the uterus and pelvis, promoting a healthy uterine environment conducive to menstruation. Additionally, licorice creams may help restore estrogen levels and balance, allowing for regular menstrual cycles to resume.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: A key compound in licorice root, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that may help alleviate menstrual cramps and discomfort associated with amenorrhea.

Flavonoids: Licorice creams contain a variety of flavonoids, such as glabridin and isoliquiritigenin, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances and improve menstrual regularity.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids like glycyrrhetinic acid and licoricidin in licorice creams exhibit estrogenic properties that may help stimulate the endometrium and promote menstrual flow in women with amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to help regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms associated with amenorrhea.
Barks: The barks contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, making it effective for relieving period pain and irregular periods.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in quantities of 2 cups per batch and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered roots with 8 ounces of cold-pressed coconut oil and 4 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler to create a smooth paste.
Add 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of aloe vera gel to the mixture and stir until well combined, taking about 5 minutes to prepare.
Pour the mixture into glass jars or tubes and store them in a cool dark place for at least 24 hours to allow the ingredients to infuse and mature, with a preparation time of approximately 1 week.
Once the cream has matured, use it as needed by gently massaging a small amount onto the abdomen or lower back areas, taking about 2-3 minutes to apply and absorb fully.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with amenorrhea because it has natural soothing properties that calm and nourish the uterine lining, promoting a healthy hormonal balance. The cream's anti-inflammatory effects also reduce pelvic congestion and alleviate menstrual cramps, allowing the body to function properly. Additionally, marshmallow creams' mucilage coating helps lubricate and protect the cervix, reducing inflammation and promoting regular menstrual cycles. This natural approach can help women overcome amenorrhea and experience a more balanced reproductive system.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow creams contain mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and protect the lining of the uterus and vaginal canal, helping to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate menstrual cramps.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The herbal extracts in marshmallow cream, such as salicin and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the reproductive tract, which can contribute to amenorrhea.

Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow creams contain antioxidant compounds like vitamin E and beta-carotene, which help protect the uterus and surrounding tissues from oxidative damage, promoting overall reproductive health and potentially helping to restore menstrual function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm vaginal inflammation and discomfort associated with amenorrhea.
Rhyzomes: Contain saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, useful in reducing menstrual cramps and pelvic pain experienced during amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh althaea officinalis leaves and flowers for optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested botanicals using shade or low-temperature drying methods within 24 hours.
Combine one part dried althaea officinalis with three parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a large mixing bowl.
Infuse the mixture by placing it in a double boiler and heating it for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally, until it reaches desired potency.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding solids and allowing the cream to cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has been shown to stimulate the hypothalamic-pituitary-ovarian axis and regulate hormonal imbalances. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat various gynecological disorders, including amenorrhea. By promoting hormonal balance, St John's Wort creams can help restore normal menstrual cycles, reducing or eliminating symptoms of amenorrhea such as irregular periods, heavy bleeding, and mood changes.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Stimulates blood flow to the uterus, helping to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce symptoms of amenorrhea such as pain and inflammation.
Hypericin: Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate pelvic pain, cramps, and discomfort associated with amenorrhea.
Flavonoids: May help regulate hormonal balance by inhibiting the breakdown of estrogen in the body, thereby helping to restore normal menstrual cycles.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St. John's Wort contain a high concentration of active compounds, including hyperforin and adhyperforin, which help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.

Flowers: The flowers of St. John's Wort are rich in flavonoids, particularly hyperoside, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the uterine lining, promoting regular menstrual cycles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and stems by cutting them at the base with scissors for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, dry place or using a food dehydrator at 60-70 degrees fahrenheit for 1-3 days.
Grind the dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder for approximately 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered hypericum perforatum with 20% beeswax and 80% coconut oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and well combined.
Fill sterilized glass containers with the cooled herbal cream mixture and store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain a unique combination of herbs that have been traditionally used to balance hormones and regulate menstrual cycles. The peony root, in particular, is believed to nourish and tonify the blood, while other ingredients like licorice root and angelica sinensis help to calm and soothe the uterus. By promoting healthy blood flow and hormonal balance, these creams can stimulate menstruation and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Estrogenic and Phytoestrogens: Herbal Chinese Peony creams contain phytoestrogens, which mimic estrogen's effects on the body, helping to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.

Anti-inflammatory and Antioxidant Properties: The creams' anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the uterus and other reproductive organs, promoting a healthy environment for menstruation.

Regulation of Hormone Secretion: Herbal Chinese Peony creams contain ingredients that help regulate hormone secretion, particularly estrogen and progesterone, which can help stimulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Chinese Peony plant have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat gynecological disorders, including amenorrhea, due to its ability to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the Chinese Peony plant are rich in saponins, which have been shown to have a positive effect on ovulation and fertility, making them a useful component for treating amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried paeonia lactiflora roots in the correct dosage of 30g for every 100ml of cream base.
Grind the dried root into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20% of the ground root powder with 80% of a neutral cream base containing ingredients like glycerin and water.
Heat the mixture at a low temperature of 50-60°c for 30 minutes to allow for infusion of herbal properties.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any impurities or sediment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain a unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins that help to stimulate blood flow and balance hormone production. The cream's potent extract from the maidenhair tree's leaves has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat menstrual irregularities, including amenorrhea. By applying the cream directly to the skin, it helps to restore a normal menstrual cycle by nourishing the endocrine system and promoting healthy ovulation. This natural remedy can provide relief for women experiencing amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or other factors.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: These properties can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which may contribute to amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or other underlying conditions.
Hormone-regulating effects: Gynostemma pentaphyllum has been shown to modulate estrogen and progesterone levels, which may help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
Stress-reducing and adaptogenic properties: This herb is known for its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can help alleviate anxiety and depression-related amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of the maidenhair tree have been traditionally used to regulate hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea.
Barks: The barks of the tree contain a unique blend of tannins and flavonoids that help to stabilize estrogen levels and promote normal menstrual cycles.
remedy_recipe: Collect and dry high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves in a clean and well-ventilated area.
Measure 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered ginkgo biloba with 20 grams of beeswax, 30 grams of coconut oil, and 50 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the ingredients are fully incorporated and the beeswax is melted.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with amenorrhea because of its unique blend of botanicals that promote hormonal balance and uterine health. The cream's active ingredients, including echinacea and black cohosh, have been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea, such as irregular bleeding or complete cessation of periods. By stimulating the production of prostaglandins and increasing blood flow to the uterus, Kansas coneflower creams can help restore a regular menstrual cycle and promote overall reproductive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects: Echinacea's flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate menstrual cramp pain and regulate hormonal imbalances associated with amenorrhea.

Immunomodulatory activity: Echinacea has been shown to modulate the immune system, potentially helping to address underlying inflammatory conditions that may contribute to amenorrhea, such as endometriosis or polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).

Hormone balancing effects: Some studies suggest that Echinacea may help balance hormonal levels, which could be beneficial in cases of amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or disorders, such as thyroid issues or polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).

Barks: Contain flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, promoting a healthy menstrual cycle.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency retention.
Combine 50g of dried echinacea angustifolia with 500ml of hot distilled water and steep for 30 minutes to create a herbal infusion.
Strain the herbal infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, retaining 300ml of the liquid.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil with the herbal infusion in a double boiler, heating until melted and well combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let cool for at least 2 hours before capping and storing in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with amenorrhea because they possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and regulate hormonal imbalances in the body. The cream's natural ingredients, such as avenanthramides and beta-glucans, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the pelvic area, which can alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea caused by conditions like endometriosis and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). Emollient and moisturizing properties: Oats creams rich in emollients like squalane, glycerin, and fatty acids can help maintain vaginal pH balance, reduce dryness and irritation, and promote healthy vaginal tissue function, potentially resolving amenorrhea related to hormonal imbalances or vaginal dryness.

Antioxidant and regenerative properties: Oats creams enriched with antioxidants like green tea, pomegranate, or grape seed extract can help protect the body from free radical damage and promote cellular regeneration, which may help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.

remedy_recipe: Grind 50 grams of dried avena sativa oatstraw flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered avena sativa with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring frequently, until the beeswax has melted completely and the temperature reaches 65°c for 15 minutes.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping the mixture into a smooth consistency using an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Pour the avena sativa cream into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at 2°c to 8°c for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with amenorrhea because they stimulate the endocrine system to regulate hormonal imbalances, which can disrupt menstruation. The cream's phytoestrogens, specifically isoflavones, bind to estrogen receptors in the body, promoting a balance between estrogen and progesterone levels. This, in turn, stimulates the uterus to thicken its lining, preparing it for a potential pregnancy, and ultimately leading to regular menstrual cycles. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties help flush out excess hormones, further supporting hormonal regulation and fertility.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion cream's diuretic properties can help stimulate blood flow and increase urine production, which may help alleviate menstrual irregularities and promote regular periods.

Hormone-balancing properties: The plant's hormone-like compounds, such as isopimaric acid, may help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to amenorrhea, promoting a return to normal menstrual cycles.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the reproductive tract and pelvic region, which can contribute to menstrual irregularities and amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots have been traditionally used to treat menstrual irregularities due to their ability to stimulate blood flow and hormone balance.
Fruits: The fruits of the dandelion plant contain compounds that help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours or air dry for 7 days.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a 40-mesh sieve to ensure consistency.
Mix 100 grams of the powdered taraxacum officinale with 300 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly for 30 minutes while heating it gently until it reaches a temperature of 80°c to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain natural compounds that stimulate hormonal balance and regulate menstruation. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce uterine cramping and spasms, which can be a common cause of amenorrhea. Additionally, stinging nettle's ability to increase estrogen levels and promote progesterone production helps restore normal menstrual cycles. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, carotenoids, and caffeic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the uterus and promote a normal menstrual cycle.

Estrogen-balancing properties: Nettle has been shown to have estrogen-balancing properties, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances that may contribute to amenorrhea. This balancing effect can also help alleviate symptoms like breast tenderness and mood swings.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing uterine inflammation and promoting regular menstrual cycles.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and dry them using a food dehydrator or air drying method for 2-3 days.
Combine dried urtica dioica leaves with carrier oils such as coconut oil or olive oil in a 1:2 ratio, blending for 30 minutes to ensure thorough extraction.
Steep the herb-infused oil mixture in a double boiler at a temperature below boiling point for 4-6 hours to extract bioactive compounds.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard solids, then add beeswax or candelilla wax at a ratio of 10% to create a semi-solid consistency.
Mix the herbal cream base with a gentle stirring motion for 2-3 minutes and fill into sterile jars, allowing it to cool and solidify before use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with amenorrhea because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has been shown to regulate hormonal balances and stimulate menstrual cycles. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into the skin allows it to target the uterine lining, promoting healthy tissue growth and thickening, which can help alleviate amenorrhea symptoms. remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea such as bloating and cramps.
Turmeric's Warming Effect: Turmeric creams have a warming effect on the body, which can help stimulate blood flow to the uterus, promoting menstrual regularity and alleviating menstrual cramp pain.
Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidant properties of turmeric help protect the body from oxidative stress, inflammation, and damage caused by hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in curcuminoids, the roots of the turmeric plant have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh turmeric rhizomes wash them thoroughly rinse with distilled water.
Dry the turmeric rhizomes in a warm place for 7-10 days or until they are brittle and easily breakable.
Grind 100 grams of dried turmeric rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the turmeric powder with 200 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low heat for 10-15 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully dissolved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with amenorrhea because it has a gentle, yet effective, ability to regulate hormonal balance in the body. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves, wash them gently and chop into small pieces.
Combine 20 grams of chopped melissa leaves with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 14 days to allow for gentle extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then mix in 10 grams of beeswax pellets with 20 milliliters of coconut oil.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof container and melt at low temperature (30-40°c) for 10-15 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried asclepias syriaca root powder from a reputable supplier.
Combine the harvested powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes over low heat, stirring occasionally, to facilitate infusion.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it steep for an additional 1 hour.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers, weighing approximately 200 grams for this recipe.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator for 1-2 hours.
Combine dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 500 milliliters of sweet almond oil and let it infuse for 2 weeks shaking occasionally.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding solids after 30 minutes.

Additionally, the creams may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for amenorrhea. By addressing these underlying issues, beach rose creams can help restore a healthy menstrual cycle and promote overall reproductive well-being.
remedy_properties: Emmenagogic properties: Beach rose creams have emmenagogic properties, which means they can stimulate menstrual flow and relieve delayed or absent menstruation (amenorrhea) by increasing uterine contractions and improving blood circulation to the reproductive organs.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in beach rose creams help relax the uterus and alleviate cramping, spasms, and discomfort associated with amenorrhea, making it easier for the body to initiate menstruation.

Estrogen-regulating properties: Beach rose creams may also help regulate estrogen levels, which can become imbalanced in cases of amenorrhea. By normalizing estrogen production, beach rose creams may help stimulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms such as mood swings, breast tenderness, and hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to regulate hormonal balance and ease menstrual cramps.
Barks: Contains phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing pelvic pain and discomfort associated with amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh rosa rugosa petals, ensuring they are free from pesticides and heavy metals.
Dry the petals in a warm oven at 50°c for 24 hours or air-dry them for 7 days.
Combine 100g of dried rosa rugosa petals with 300ml of organic coconut oil and infuse for 14 days.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth, retaining the oil and discarding the solids, taking approximately 30 minutes.
Mix the strained oil with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter, heating at 60°c until smooth and creamy.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for amenorrhea are topical preparations made from natural herbs and plant extracts that help to stimulate menstruation and alleviate symptoms associated with menstrual irregularities. These creams work by promoting hormonal balance, relaxing uterine muscles, and increasing blood flow to the pelvic area, which can help to induce regular periods. Examples of herbal creams that may be effective in treating amenorrhea include Clary Sage, Dong Quai, and Chasteberry cream, which have been used for centuries to promote menstrual health and alleviate symptoms such as cramping and mood swings.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat amenorrhea with herbal creams is to identify the underlying cause, which could be hormonal imbalance, stress, or nutrient deficiencies. Herbal creams containing chasteberry, dong quai, and black cohosh have been found to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea. Apply a thin layer of the cream to the lower abdomen twice daily for 2-3 months, ensuring consistent absorption and effectiveness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to amenorrhea that are treated with herbal creams are endometriosis, polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), and dysmenorrhea. Herbal creams containing ingredients like chasteberry, Dong quai, and wild yam help alleviate symptoms such as irregular periods, heavy bleeding, and pelvic pain by promoting hormonal balance and improving uterine health. These creams can be used in conjunction with conventional treatments to provide natural relief from menstrual disorders.
